dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal identifies volume spikes (2x normal volume) as context-dependent signals marking potential end of significant price moves. Used vol_ratio_20 to detect volume spike relative to 20-day average. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as the signal itself states the move end could be bullish or bearish depending on preceding price action. Entry triggers on spike detection; exit when volume normalizes below 1.5x threshold. The observation period constraint (>1 month, ideally >3 months) is implicitly captured by vol_ratio_20's 20-day lookback window, though a longer window would be ideal for this signal.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal identifies volume spikes (2x normal volume) as context-dependent signals marking potential end of significant price moves. Used vol_ratio_20 to detect volume spike relative to 20-day average. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as the signal itself states the move end could be bullish or bearish depending on preceding price action. Entry triggers on spike detection; exit when volume normalizes below 1.5x threshold. The observation period constraint (>1 month, ideally >3 months) is implicitly captured by vol_ratio_20's 20-day lookback window, though a longer window would be ideal for this signal.

source_rule_text: Identify a volume spike when volume is at least twice that of other days over a period longer than one month (most likely longer than three months), as it is normally associated with the end of a significant price move.
